diagnostics.title: Large component is hard to read and change
diagnostics.message: Component "PatientDetailsPage" is 541 lines long, which is hard to read & change. Split it into a few smaller components.
diagnostics.help: Pull each section into its own component so the parent is easier to read, test, and change.

diagnostics.title: Large component is hard to read and change
diagnostics.message: Component "SchoolDetailsPage" is 310 lines long, which is hard to read & change. Split it into a few smaller components.
diagnostics.help: Pull each section into its own component so the parent is easier to read, test, and change.

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<button>` than `role="button"`, so use `<button>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: Boolean prop combinations are hard to test
diagnostics.message: Component "ProviderConfirmationModals" takes 6 on/off props (isAddingSchool, isRemovingSchool, isAddingPartnerΓÇª), which is hard to combine & test. Split it into smaller components or named variants.
diagnostics.help: Split boolean-heavy APIs into smaller components or named variants so combinations stay testable.

diagnostics.title: Boolean prop combinations are hard to test
diagnostics.message: Component "PartnerConfirmationModals" takes 4 on/off props (isAddingSchool, isRemovingSchool, isAddingProviderΓÇª), which is hard to combine & test. Split it into smaller components or named variants.
diagnostics.help: Split boolean-heavy APIs into smaller components or named variants so combinations stay testable.

diagnostics.title: Plain img ships unoptimized images

diagnostics.help: Use `next/image` so users get optimized formats, responsive srcsets, and lazy loading instead of oversized image downloads.

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<button>` than `role="button"`, so use `<button>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: State initialized from a mount effect
diagnostics.message: Your users see an extra render with empty "mounted" because a useEffect sets its starting value.
diagnostics.help: Pass the initial value directly to useState() instead of setting it from a mount-only useEffect. For SSR hydration, prefer useSyncExternalStore().

diagnostics.title: useEffect setState flashes on mount
diagnostics.message: This flashes for your users because useEffect(setState, []) runs after the first paint, so use useSyncExternalStore, or add suppressHydrationWarning
diagnostics.help: Use `useSyncExternalStore(subscribe, getSnapshot, getServerSnapshot)` or add `suppressHydrationWarning` to the element

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<button>` than `role="button"`, so use `<button>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: Array index used as a key
diagnostics.message: Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "idx".
diagnostics.help: Use a stable id from the item, like `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}`. Index keys break when the list reorders or filters.

diagnostics.title: Component rendered by inline function call
diagnostics.message: Your users lose state because "renderActions()" builds UI from an inline call that React remounts, so pull it into its own component instead.
diagnostics.help: Make it a named component so React preserves its identity and does not remount its state.

diagnostics.title: Boolean prop combinations are hard to test

diagnostics.title: Prefer explicit variant components
diagnostics.message: Component "NotificationsSheet" picks which component to render from 2 boolean props (isMarkingAllRead, isClearing), which multiplies untestable variants. Split it into explicit variant components so each renders one clear path.
diagnostics.help: Replace boolean props that switch whole subtrees with explicit variant components, like `<ThreadComposer />` and `<EditMessageComposer />`, so each variant renders one clear path.

diagnostics.title: Client-side redirect for navigation

diagnostics.title: Repeated localStorage reads
diagnostics.message: This is slow because localStorage.getItem("recoveryToken") runs several times & re-parses the data each call, so read it once & reuse the value
diagnostics.help: Read `localStorage`/`sessionStorage` once and reuse the value. Every read has to parse the data again, which is slow

diagnostics.title: Derived value copied into state
diagnostics.message: Storing "internalValue" in state when you can derive it from other values costs an extra render.
diagnostics.help: Work out the value while rendering (or with useMemo if it's expensive) instead of copying it into useState through a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#updating-state-based-on-props-or-state

diagnostics.title: Event logic handled in an effect
diagnostics.message: Faking an event handler with a prop plus a useEffect costs an extra render & runs late.
diagnostics.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

diagnostics.title: Plain img ships unoptimized images
diagnostics.message: Plain ships unoptimized, oversized images.
diagnostics.help: Use `next/image` so users get optimized formats, responsive srcsets, and lazy loading instead of oversized image downloads.

diagnostics.title: Multiple setState calls in one effect
diagnostics.message: 3 setState calls in one useEffect redraw your screen each time they run together.
diagnostics.help: Combine related updates in `useReducer` so one effect does not redraw the screen once per `setState` call.

diagnostics.title: State only used in handlers
diagnostics.message: Each update to "cycleCollisionDetected" redraws your component for nothing because this useState is set but never shown on screen.
diagnostics.help: Use useRef instead of useState when the value is only set and never shown on screen. `ref.current = ...` updates it without redrawing the component.

diagnostics.title: Subscribing to raw query atom
diagnostics.message: `useAtom(searchQueryAtom)` subscribes to the whole query atom, so it re-renders your component on every refetch, focus, or no-op cache hit. Derive the field first: `const dataAtom = atom((get) => get(searchQueryAtom).data)`.
diagnostics.help: Derive the field you read: `const dataAtom = atom((get) => get(queryAtom).data)`. Subscribing to the whole query atom re-renders on every refetch, focus, or no-op cache hit.

diagnostics.title: Subscribing to raw query atom
diagnostics.message: `useAtom(appliedSearchQueryAtom)` subscribes to the whole query atom, so it re-renders your component on every refetch, focus, or no-op cache hit. Derive the field first: `const dataAtom = atom((get) => get(appliedSearchQueryAtom).data)`.
diagnostics.help: Derive the field you read: `const dataAtom = atom((get) => get(queryAtom).data)`. Subscribing to the whole query atom re-renders on every refetch, focus, or no-op cache hit.

diagnostics.title: Listener re-subscribes on every handler change

diagnostics.message: Component "ProviderNotificationsSheet" takes 5 on/off props (isMarkingRead, isDeleting, isMarkingAllReadΓÇª), which is hard to combine & test. Split it into smaller components or named variants.
diagnostics.help: Split boolean-heavy APIs into smaller components or named variants so combinations stay testable.

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<button>` than `role="button"`, so use `<button>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: Component rendered by inline function call

diagnostics.title: Prefer explicit variant components
diagnostics.message: Component "NotificationsSheet" picks which component to render from 2 boolean props (isMarkingAllRead, isClearing), which multiplies untestable variants. Split it into explicit variant components so each renders one clear path.
diagnostics.help: Replace boolean props that switch whole subtrees with explicit variant components, like `<ThreadComposer />` and `<EditMessageComposer />`, so each variant renders one clear path.

diagnostics.title: Derived value copied into state
diagnostics.message: Storing "internalValue" in state when you can derive it from other values costs an extra render.
diagnostics.help: Work out the value while rendering (or with useMemo if it's expensive) instead of copying it into useState through a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#updating-state-based-on-props-or-state

diagnostics.title: Event logic handled in an effect
diagnostics.message: Faking an event handler with a prop plus a useEffect costs an extra render & runs late.
diagnostics.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

diagnostics.title: Plain img ships unoptimized images
diagnostics.message: Plain ships unoptimized, oversized images.
diagnostics.help: Use `next/image` so users get optimized formats, responsive srcsets, and lazy loading instead of oversized image downloads.

diagnostics.title: Multiple setState calls in one effect
diagnostics.message: 3 setState calls in one useEffect redraw your screen each time they run together.
diagnostics.help: Combine related updates in `useReducer` so one effect does not redraw the screen once per `setState` call.

diagnostics.title: State only used in handlers
diagnostics.message: Each update to "cycleCollisionDetected" redraws your component for nothing because this useState is set but never shown on screen.
diagnostics.help: Use useRef instead of useState when the value is only set and never shown on screen. `ref.current = ...` updates it without redrawing the component.

diagnostics.title: Subscribing to raw query atom
diagnostics.message: `useAtom(searchQueryAtom)` subscribes to the whole query atom, so it re-renders your component on every refetch, focus, or no-op cache hit. Derive the field first: `const dataAtom = atom((get) => get(searchQueryAtom).data)`.
diagnostics.help: Derive the field you read: `const dataAtom = atom((get) => get(queryAtom).data)`. Subscribing to the whole query atom re-renders on every refetch, focus, or no-op cache hit.

diagnostics.help: Derive the field you read: `const dataAtom = atom((get) => get(queryAtom).data)`. Subscribing to the whole query atom re-renders on every refetch, focus, or no-op cache hit.
